item.title: Cross Atlas Remapping via Optimal Transport (CAROT): Creating connectomes for different atlases when raw data is not available
item.description: Abstract Open-source, publicly available neuroimaging datasets—whether from large-scale data collection efforts or pooled from multiple smaller studies—offer unprecedented sample sizes and promote generalization efforts.

item.title: Altered brain dynamics across bipolar disorder and schizophrenia revealed by overlapping brain states
item.description: Abstract Aberrant brain dynamics putatively characterize bipolar disorder (BD) and schizophrenia (SCZ).

item.title: Predicting the future of neuroimaging predictive models in mental health
item.description: Abstract Predictive modeling using neuroimaging data has the potential to improve our understanding of the neurobiology underlying psychiatric disorders and putatively information interventions.

item.title: Method and system for information retrieval
item.description: Abstract A perspective-based search system and method is disclosed. The perspective based system allows users to select specific perspectives on a search topic and view the results from those perspectives.

item.title: Trustworthy ML & Robust Statistics
item.description: As machine learning systems are pervasively deployed in many scientific fields with increasingly sensitive tasks, it has become paramount to develop algorithms that are robust against the numerous sources of uncertainty inherent in those applications including noise in the data, malicious exploitation of vulnerabilities, outliers, variability of the true objective, privacy, and fairness.

item.title: An expectation-maximization algorithm for query translation based on pseudo-relevant documents
item.description: Abstract Query translation in cross-language information retrieval (CLIR) can be done by employing dictionaries, aligned corpora, or machine translators.

item.title: Building a multi-domain comparable corpus using a learning to rank method
item.description: Abstract Comparable corpora are key translation resources for both languages and domains with limited linguistic resources.

item.title: Stacking multiple optimal transport policies to map functional connectomes
item.description: Abstract Connectomics is a popular approach for understanding the brain with neuroimaging data.

item.title: Combining multiple atlases to estimate data-driven mappings between functional connectomes using optimal transport
item.description: Abstract Connectomics is a popular approach for understanding the brain with neuroimaging data.

item.title: Transforming connectomes to any parcellation via graph matching
item.description: Abstract Brain connectomes—the structural or functional connections between distinct brain regions—are widely used for neuroimaging stud- ies.

item.title: Data-driven mapping between functional connectomes using optimal transport
item.description: Abstract Functional connectomes derived from functional magnetic resonance imaging have long been used to understand the functional organization of the brain.

item.title: A mass multivariate edge-wise approach for combining multiple connectomes to improve the detection of group differences | Best Poster in Connectomics in NeuroImage
item.description: Abstract Functional connectivity derived from functional magnetic resonance imaging data has been extensively used to characterize individual and group differences.

item.title: Dimension projection among languages based on pseudo-relevant documents for query translation
item.description: Abstract Using top-ranked documents in response to a query has been shown to be an effective approach to improve the quality of query translation in dictionary- based cross-language information retrieval.

item.title: Learning to Weight Translations using Ordinal Linear Regression and Query-generated Training Data for Ad-hoc Retrieval with Long Queries
item.description: Abstract Ordinal regression which is known with learning to rank has long been used in information re- trieval (IR).

item.title: Pseudo-relevance feedback based on matrix factorization
item.description: Abstract In information retrieval, pseudo-relevance feedback (PRF) refers to a strategy for updating the query model using the top retrieved documents.

item.title: SS4MCT: a statistical stemmer for morphologically complex texts
item.description: Abstract There have been multiple attempts to resolve various inflec- tion matching problems in information retrieval.

item.title: CAROT appears in Yale News!
item.description: Yale researchers encourage brain data reuse with CAROT Maps of brain connectivity are built on brain atlases, of which there are many.

item.title: Talk in Johns Hopkings University
item.description: I will be preseneting our recent study on the potential benefits of using stacked policies to accelerate the process of transferring connectomes between atlases.

item.title: Talk in Massachusetts General Hospital
item.description: I will give a talk in Massachussetts General Hospital, hosted by Bruce Fischl in March 15.

item.title: Talk in MIT (Ev's lab)
item.description: I will present CAROT at Ev’s language lab at McGovern Institute for Brain Research.

item.title: Talk in Boston Children Hospital
item.description: I will give a talk in Boton Children’s Hospital, hosted by Ali Gholipour in Jan 15.

item.title: Enhancement attacks in biomedical machine learning
item.description: Abstract The prevalence of machine learning in biomedical research is rapidly growing, yet the trustworthiness of such research is often over- looked.

item.title: Best Paper Award at Graphs in Biomedical Image Analysis
item.description: Our paper entitled “Transforming connectomes to “any” parcellation via graph matching” by Qinghao Liang et.

item.title: TILOS annual retreat
item.description: I will participate at TILOS Annual Retreat in San Diego (Wednesday, October 19 – Friday, October 21).

item.title: 1KD PI meeting
item.description: I will participate at 1KD PI meeting between Sep 7- Sep 10 at San Francisco.

item.title: I am in job market
item.description: I am in my final year of grad school and actively looking for jobs.

item.title: Cross Atlas Remapping via Optimal Transport (CAROT): Creating connectomes for any atlas when raw data is not available
item.description: Abstract Whether using large-scale projects—like the Human Connectome Project (HCP), the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study, Healthy Brain Network (HBN), and the UK Biobank—or pooling together several smaller studies, open-source, publicly available datasets allow for unpresented sample sizes and promote generalization efforts.

item.title: Sequence Models and Recurrent Neural Networks
item.description: Before talking about recurrent neural networks, let’s talk about hidden Markov models (HMM) first.

item.title: Carot is accepted for MICCAI 2022
item.description: Our paper entitled “Combining multiple atlases to estimate data-driven mappings between functional connectomes using optimal transport” has been provisionally accepted for MICCAI 2022.

item.title: Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Reinforcement Learning is the third category of big topics in machine learning after supervised learning and unsupervised learning.

item.title: Sparsity and Graphs
item.description: Graphs allow us to encode structural assumptions about data. Graphs are the natural language for describing all kinds of problems and data.

item.title: Variational Inference and VAE
item.description: In the previous post, we talked about approximation inference. We want to compute $p(\theta,z|x)$, but it’s too complicated.

item.title: Approximation Inference
item.description: In the previous post, we talked about Gibbs sampling and posterior inference.

item.title: Carot is launched
item.description: We are happy to announce that carotproject.com is now available online.

item.title: Gibbs Sampling
item.description: Review: In the previous post, we talked about the Dirichlet process and Dirichlet process mixture, as the Dirichlet process is for CDF estimation and the Dirichlet process mixture is for density estimation (i.

item.title: Dirichlet Processes
item.description: Review: In the previous post, we talked about Bayesian Inference and Gaussian processes.

item.title: Gaussian Processes
item.description: Bayesian Inference The parameter $\theta$ in Bayesian Inference is viewed as a random variable.

item.title: Neural Tangent Kernels
item.description: Let’s start with a simple regression method. Let’s assume that we have a dataset of $n$ points ${(x_i,y_i)}_{i=1}^n$ where $y_i \in \mathbb{R}$ and $x_i \in \mathbb{R}^d$:

item.title: Representer Theorem
item.description: In the previous post, we talked about Mercer’s theorem and defined a mercer kernel as $\int f(x) f(y) k(x,y) dx dy \geq 0$ for any function $f$.

item.title: Mercer's Theorem
item.description: Given data points $(X_1,Y_1)$ , $(X_2,Y_2)$ , .. , and $(X_n,Y_n)$ where we want to predict $Y$ via design matrix $X$.

item.title: Sparsity Meets Convexity
item.description: Here we will talk about basic concepts of regression in sparse data.

item.title: Expectation Maximization
item.description: We discussed the EM algorithm on Gaussian Mixture models in the previous post.

item.title: How to Manage 100k+ Experiments on Brain Imaging Datasets
item.description: Ph.D. students studying machine learning on the human brain must conduct massive experiments on brain imaging datasets.

item.title: Mixture Models and EM
item.description: How to model data with a probability distribution? For example, if data looks like a circle or symmetric, we may model it with a Gaussian distribution:

item.title: Posterior Inference
item.description: Bayesian Inference what can we do with posterior distribution $p(\theta|x)$ that we can not do with point estimate: For example we can estimate by $\theta_{\text{MAP}} = \arg \max_{\theta} p(\theta|x)$

item.title: Empirical Risk Minimization
item.description: Empirical Risk Minimization: Given $(x_1,y_1), .., (x_n,y_n) \in X \times y$ and we want to predict $f(x)=y$.

item.title: Maximum Likelihood and Maximum A Posteriori Estimation
item.description: Maximum Likelihood Estimation: Suppose that we have a probabilistic model of generating random data $x \in \mathcal{X}$ derived from function $P_{\theta}(x)$ that is parametrized by $\theta$.

item.title: Write your first stochastic function in Python
item.description: Let’s estimate how many ice creams insomnia cookies in New Haven will sell this Fall.

item.title: Learning Optimal Transport from Samples
item.description: Searching for optimal transport encourages a mapping that minimizes the total cost of transporting mass from $\alpha$ to $\beta$, as initially formulated by Monge (1781).

item.title: Let's touch base on kernel methods
item.description: Empirical risk minimization with linear model $f(x) = w.x$ given data points is a traditional way of learning coefficients $w$.

item.title: Optimal Transport and Brain Imaging
item.description: Fuctional connectomes, derived from functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) is widely used to study functional organization of human brain.

item.title: A hitchhiker’s guide to working with large, open-source neuroimaging datasets
item.description: Abstract Large datasets that enable researchers to perform investigations with unprecedented rigor are growing increasingly common in neuroimaging.

item.title: Functional Connectivity During Frustration: Predictive Modeling of Irritability in Youth
item.description: Abstract Irritability cuts across many pediatric disorders and is a common presenting complaint in child psychiatry; however, its neural mechanisms remain unclear.

item.title: Transdiagnostic, Connectome-Based Prediction of Memory Constructs Across Psychiatric Disorders
item.description: Abstract Memory deficits are observed in a range of psychiatric disorders, but it is unclear whether memory deficits arise from a shared brain correlate across disorders or from various dysfunctions unique to each disorder.

item.title: Predicting BMI From Whole-Brain Functional Connectivity
item.description: Abstract Variations in body habitus (eg obesity) have been linked to widespread changes in the brain.

item.title: iid.yale.edu is launched
item.description: We are happy to announce that iid.yale.edu is now available online.

item.title: Collaborative Machine Learning & Federated Learning
item.description: The human brain is a complex network of functionally interconnected regions whose coordinated effort gives rise to different functions.

item.title: Functional Connectivity
item.description: The human brain is a complex network of functionally interconnected regions whose coordinated effort gives rise to different functions.

item.title: Sequence to Logic with Copy and Cache
item.description: Abstract Generating logical form equivalents of human language is a fresh way to employ neural architectures where long short-term memory effectively captures dependencies in both encoder and decoder units.

item.title: Zero-shot transfer learning for semantic parsing
item.description: Abstract While neural networks have shown impressive performance on large datasets, applying these models to tasks where little data is available remains a challenging problem.

item.title: Biography
item.description: My early childhood was spent in the west of Iran in a beautiful city named Bonab.
